symptoms.symptom_name: High-energy mechanism (fall down stairs) with an audible crack at impact

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Open displaced fracture of the tibia
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A high-energy bending injury is a typical mechanism for a displaced tibial shaft fracture and argues against a pathological fracture.
Adequate energy to break healthy bone makes a pathological (low-energy) fracture unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Gross angular deformity of the mid-lower leg

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Open displaced fracture of the tibia
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Visible angulation indicates a displaced cortical break; patient/video-observable so admissible via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe soft-tissue injury / ligamentous sprain of the lower leg
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sprain does not produce fixed angular deformity of the shaft.

symptoms.symptom_name: Open wound with bone visible through the skin over the fracture

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Open displaced fracture of the tibia
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Visible bone through a wound over the fracture site defines an open (compound) fracture — the feature that makes it a surgical emergency.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Open displaced fracture of the tibia
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable crepitus confirms a cortical break — a clinician examination finding, not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Complete loss of function — unable to bear weight or move the ankle

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Open displaced fracture of the tibia
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Total inability to weight-bear or move the joint below the injury is consistent with a significant displaced fracture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe localised bony tenderness and swelling with early ecchymosis

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Open displaced fracture of the tibia
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Focal bony tenderness, swelling and bruising accompany a fresh shaft fracture and haematoma.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute compartment syndrome of the leg
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Progressive swelling is the substrate for compartment syndrome; tense swelling with disproportionate pain would raise concern.

symptoms.symptom_name: Nausea, lightheadedness, pallor and sweating (pain-related autonomic response)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Open displaced fracture of the tibia
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A vasovagal/pain response is expected with severe pain; also prompts vigilance for evolving haemodynamic compromise.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia (HR 118) and mildly elevated blood pressure secondary to pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Open displaced fracture of the tibia
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured tachycardia is consistent with severe pain; a rising tachycardia would also flag blood loss. Sealed clinician vital, not obtainable in this telehealth encounter.

symptoms.symptom_name: Distal foot colour, warmth, sensation and movement

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A carer can be guided to report whether the foot is pink, warm and sensate; a change to pale/cold/numb signals vascular injury or compartment syndrome and mandates immediate escalation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute compartment syndrome of the leg
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Late compartment syndrome produces sensory loss and pulselessness; carer monitoring of the foot is the telehealth safety-net proxy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intact distal pulses (dorsalis pedis, posterior tibial), capillary refill under 2 seconds and intact light-touch sensation at presentation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reassuring pertinent negatives at this moment — but a snapshot only; clinician-measured and not available via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Plain radiographs showing cortical disruption and displacement of bone fragments

investigation_details.investigation_name: Orthogonal plain radiographs of the tibia/fibula (including joint above and below)
investigation_details.expected_result: Displaced mid-shaft tibial fracture with cortical disruption
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Fracture configuration and articular involvement not characterised — but imaging must not delay emergency escalation and surgical care.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Open displaced fracture of the tibia
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Radiographs confirm cortical break and displacement; a hospital investigation, never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Open displaced fracture of the tibia
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Angular deformity + bone visible through a wound + bony crepitus + complete loss of function together are diagnostic of an open displaced fracture and define a surgical emergency.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Orthogonal plain radiographs of the tibia and fibula (joint above and below)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms fracture configuration and displacement in hospital — but is performed after arrival and must not delay 000 escalation or splinting.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Neurovascular assessment of the foot (pulses, capillary refill, sensation, motor) — serial
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Limb-threat surveillance for vascular injury and compartment syndrome; the telehealth proxy is guided carer observation of the foot.
If evolving pain out of proportion or a tense compartment develops in hospital; not obtainable pre-hospital or via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Whole-leg CT / angiography for arterial injury
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not a pre-hospital or telehealth decision; ordered by the trauma team only if vascular injury is suspected on arrival — the AI Doctor should not attempt to arrange advanced imaging.
